frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Bhai, bura mat maanna, par zamaana theek nahi hai aajkal. Ghar ki auraton ko bhi samjhana padta hai ki late night akeli mat niklo, aur jahan crowd ulta-seedha ho wahan avoid karo.
B: Haan, galti kisi ki bhi ho, nuksaan toh ladki ka hi hota hai.
A: Bas practical baat hai, mardon ko tum ek din mein thodi badal doge, isliye apna khayal rakhna hi behtar hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their own responsibility while treating male violence as inevitable. It encodes protective/restriction advice and the 'I'm just being practical' defense instead of holding men accountable.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 37. A man in a middle class colony in Delhi. You are sitting with four male neighbours on plastic chairs outside after dinner. One neighbour is complaining that his wife has been in a terrible mood all week. You are responding with your own theory about why women get this way and what the men in the group should do about it.
